title: Acmella ramosa
intro: Acmella ramosa, also known as paracress or Brazilian pepper cress, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the relief of pain and inflammation, as well as promote wound healing. Acmella ramosa contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Acmella ramosa is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including oral health issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Acmella ramosa is classified in the family Asteraceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships. Within the Asteraceae family, Acmella ramosa is placed in the tribe Heliantheae. The plant is further classified as belonging to the genus Acmella, which includes species commonly known as 'buzz buttons'. Acmella ramosa is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
uses: Acmella ramosa is known for its potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant's extracts have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in treating various health issues. Some research suggests that Acmella ramosa may have analgesic and antiseptic effects, making it a promising natural remedy for certain conditions. However, more extensive studies are needed to fully understand its medicinal applications and potential benefits. Further research is required to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Acmella ramosa for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Acmella ramosa, also known as spilanthes, has been traditionally used in folk medicine, but its side effects are not well-documented. Additionally, Acmella ramosa may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and decrease their effectiveness.
